title: Fake Blocks
description: A simple datapack that allows you to create fake blocks
body:
## Fake Blocks

This is a simple datapack that allows you to place fake blocks. These blocks look like normal blocks (most of the time), but they don't have collision, which means you can pass through them. This can be used to add secrets or troll parts to maps.

## How to use

Take the block that you want to create a fake version of into your primary hand. Then, run the `/function 
fake_blocks:place`﻿ command.

To delete a fake block, run the `/function fake_blocks:remove` command.

## Q&A

> Version compatibility?

Theoretically should work on 1.14+, but I haven't tested it.

> Block X looks broken or weird

It's likely caused by the way falling_block entites are rendered, and I probably can't do anything about it.

> Can I disable/remove the datapack after I'm done placing all the fake blocks in my map?

If the datapack is not enabled, all the fake blocks will break after being loaded for 2147484248 ticks, which is roughly 3.5 real life years. This is (probably) not an issue for you, so you can safely disable or remove the datapack after you're done using it.
